content.full_text:
CAPITOL OFFICE
STATE CAPITOL
P.O. BOX 942849
SACRAMENTO, CA 94249-0001
(916) 445-7550

Assembly
California Legislature

DISTRICT OFFICE
16145 E WHITTIER BLVD.
WHITTIER, CA 90603
(213) 947-9878

PAUL V. HORCHER
ASSEMBLYMAN, FIFTY-SECOND DISTRICT

Serving the cities & communities of Whittier, Hacienda Heights,
La Mirada, La Habra Heights, Rowland Heights, Walnut, Diamond Bar, West Covina

January 31, 1991

Mr. Mark Morogami
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mark:

     I wanted to restate the situation on VIP parking
at Ontario Airport as per our phone conversation on
January 31, 1991. With the new law limiting gifts, I am
unsure of how to report the usage of the lot.
     I spoke with Sandi Interlichchia and Shirley
Schnell of Ontario Airport management to try to
establish a value for the VIP parking pass made
available to Assemblyman Horcher. I was informed that
the parking lot is monitored on a daily basis by
recording the license plate numbers. They then report
monthly to the City of Los Angeles on who has used the
lot, and assess them a fee equal to eight dollars per
day.
     At Los Angeles City Hall, I spoke with Helen
Gibson. She told me that she keeps files on the used of
the VIP lot, and then bills the individuals at the end
of the year. She also told me that some legislators pay
their yearly parking bill from their campaign funds.
     From what I understand after talking with you, it
is appropriate to use campaign funds for official
business or campaign business. As long as the parking
lot is not being used for personal business, i.e.
vacations, there would not be a problem if the campaign
committee paid this bill.
     Please let me know if there is any disagreement
between your interpretation of the situation and mine.

Sincerely,

Kimberly Schuld
Legislative Aide

Printed on Recycled Paper

embedding.qa_text: This letter is from Assemblyman Paul V. Horcher, representing the 52nd District of California, to Mr. Mark Morogami of the Fair Political Practices Commission. The letter discusses the situation regarding VIP parking at Ontario Airport and the new law limiting gifts. Horcher expresses uncertainty about how to report the usage of the lot and provides information on how the parking lot is monitored and assessed for fees. He also mentions that some legislators pay their yearly parking bill from their campaign funds. Horcher concludes by asking if there is any disagreement between his interpretation of the situation and Morogami's. January 31, 1991 Mr. Mark Morogami Fair Political Practices Commission 428 J Street, Suite 800 Sacramento, CA 95814 Dear Mark: I wanted to restate the situation on VIP parking at Ontario Airport as per our phone conversation on January 31, 1991. With the new law limiting gifts, I am unsure of how to report the usage of the lot. I spoke with Sandi Interlicchia and Shirley Schnell of Ontario Airport management to try to establish a value for the VIP parking pass made available to Assemblyman Horcher. I was informed that the parking lot is monitored on a daily basis by recording the license plate numbers. They then report monthly to the City of Los Angeles on who has used the lot, and assess them a fee equal to eight dollars per day. At Los Angeles City Hall, I spoke with Helen Gibson. She told me that she keeps files on the use of the VIP lot, and then bills the individuals at the end of the year. She also told me that some legislators pay their yearly parking bill from their campaign funds. From what I understand after talking with you, it is appropriate to use campaign funds for official business or campaign business. As long as the parking lot is not being used for personal business, i.e. vacations, there would not be a problem if the campaign committee paid this bill. Please let me know if there is any disagreement between your interpretation of the situation and mine. Sincerely, Kimberly Schuld Legislative Aide
